hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: If $$$a_1 + \cdots + a_n = 0$$$, then the answer is NO.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: If $$$a_1 + \cdots + a_n \not=0$$$, then the answer is YES.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: If $$$a_1 + \cdots + a_n \gt 0$$$, then there is a rearrangement such that all prefix sums are strictly positive.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: The score is $$$2\cdot\texttt{#{wins}} - \texttt{#{winning_streaks}}$$$.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: You shall minimize the number of disjoint winning streaks.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Fill the holes between winning streaks.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Find an $$$O(n^2)$$$ dynamic-programming solution.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: The city is relatively small (i.e., $$$r\le 500$$$).

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: If $$$j \gt i + 2r$$$ then it is possible to go from the $$$i$$$-th appearance to the $$$j$$$-th appearance. Use this observation to optimize the $$$O(n^2)$$$ solution to $$$O(nr)$$$.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: If two consecutive cards have consecutive numbers (in the right order) then you can "merge them".

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Consider only moves with small $$$k$$$.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Find one move that increases by at least $$$1$$$ the number of adjacent cards with consecutive numbers.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Write a number $$$y$$$ coprime with $$$x$$$.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Write the gcd between $$$x$$$ and $$$y$$$ (that is $$$1$$$).

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Bezout's Theorem.

hints.title: Hint 4
hints.content: If $$$a = b+1$$$ and $$$b$$$ is even, then $$$a\wedge b=1$$$.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Keep a basis of the numbers that are the xor of a subset of the numbers on the blackboard (i.e., the xor-subspace generated by numbers on the blackboard).

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Make some random choices.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: If $$$x=2^{19} + 1$$$, then it is necessary to write a number $$$ \gt 10^{11}$$$ on the blackboard.

hints.title: Hint 4
hints.content: Choose randomly two numbers from the xor-subspace and write their sum on the blackboard.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Solve the problem without the constraint of alternating turns.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: The final situation is achievable if and only if it is achievable without alternating turns but with Giada taking the last turn.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Use a greedy approach to take care of the alternating turns.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Solve the problem when the already painted tiles have only two shades.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Formulate the problem as many min-cuts problems.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Update the max-flow repeatedly to compute quickly enough the many min-cuts.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Formulate the problem as a linear-programming problem.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Compute the dual of the problem.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Use the shortest-augmenting-path algorithm to compute the min-cost maxflow and optimize Dijkstra as much as possible (and simplify the graph as much as possible).

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Solve the problem with one guard instead of two.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: If the prisoner can escape, his strategy is easy.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: If the prisoner can escape, then he should go to a point on the climbable walls and either escape immediately or go toward one of two different points on the climbable walls and escape there.

hints.title: Hint 4
hints.content: The prisoner can escape if and only if there are three points A < B < C on the climbable walls such that a guard cannot go from B to A as fast as the prisoner and a guard cannot go from B to C as fast as the prisoner.

hints.title: Hint 5
hints.content: Binary search on the answer.

hints.title: Hint 6
hints.content: Fix the sides where A and B are. The points B such that there is a good point A form an interval, determine the interval.

hints.title: Hint 7
hints.content: Be optimistic and use ternary-search (or be less optimistic and solve a bunch of quadratic equations).
